---
title: "xileh"
---

**xileh** is a lightweight, composable pipeline abstraction built around a
hierarchical data container. It generalizes the `sklearn.Pipeline` idea beyond
a feature matrix `X` and target `y` to arbitrary, nested data structures.

The library is built from two components:

- [`xData`](docs/api/xData.qmd) — a hierarchical container holding *data*, a
  *header* (description / control flags), and *meta* information.
- [`xPipeline`](docs/api/xPipeline.qmd) — an ordered set of plain Python
  functions that each take and return an `xData` object.

## Design philosophy

`xileh` deliberately aims to:

- impose as few restrictions on your workflow as possible (arbitrary data
  objects and plain Python functions),
- integrate easily with a function-based workflow during development,
- provide a single source of truth for the processing,
- enable reuse of whole pipelines by strongly motivating composition.

Take `sklearn.Pipeline` as the motivating example: it is great for building,
storing, and evaluating ML pipelines, but it is specific about the shape of
data it processes and how (`fit` / `transform`). `xileh` trades built-in
functionality for maximal freedom: a single object describing the full
pipeline, over any data.

## Where to next?

- [Installation](docs/guide/installation.qmd)
- [Quick Start](docs/guide/quickstart.qmd)
- [Examples](docs/examples/index.qmd)
- [API Reference](docs/api/index.qmd)
